ksa_text: Knowledge of dbt and transformation framework patterns, including model layering strategies (staging, intermediate, mart layers), testing approaches, documentation generation, and automated lineage creation.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Data Architecture & Analytics KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.405) to justify inclusion for the AI CoE Leader. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world AI CoE Leader roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Data Architecture & Analytics is not a standard competency expectation for AI CoE Leader. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the AI CoE Leader competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.405) and edge-domain placement (Data Architecture & Analytics) do not meet inclusion threshold for the AI CoE Leader. All passes support exclusion.
